schemaSha256Note: re-hashed 2026-07-11 over the carried studiobox schema/ bundle (renamed identifiers; limabox baseline was 617920c8753001a23873f850934fd2a6ebb3b964f81f4ce4a7504ab6d92fbe48)

toolchain.commitNote: the 0.4.0 release commit (runtime-only vs 0.3.0: capability-retention/refcount fix, emitter unchanged so bindings are byte-identical); capnpc-deno is not shipped in the JSR package, so the sibling checkout is the codegen toolchain of record

codegen.probeStatus: regenerated with the full six-schema set at the 0.3.0 adoption on 2026-07-11; byte-identical to the committed src/wire/generated/ bindings and typechecks strict against the published runtime
codegen.observedAgainst: capnp-deno checkout 0d053a20b1d42da218464a018e34f8274d959f6f (the published 0.3.0): 0.2.0's cross-file/imports/RpcStub<T>/namespaced-barrel behavior carries forward; 0.3.0 additionally forwards schema-defined close methods through stub lifecycle proxies and registers context exports wire-managed (referenceCount 0, one wire ref per Return capTable occurrence), and its WASM session core relays Return frames naming freshly host-minted exports (the M3 agent-plane blocker)
codegen.blockerNote: HISTORICAL: the 0.1.0-era blockers were all resolved by capnp-deno 0.2.0 (adopted 2026-07-11). For provenance, the qualified 0.1.0 inventory was: (1) cross-file struct/enum references emitted bare unimported TypeScript names (SbxError, EmptyResult, TransferCommit, FinishResult, ProtocolOffer, KeyValue, HandshakeResult, AuthResult, ReadResult; 159 TS2304 errors) with runtime descriptors lowered to TYPE_ANY_POINTER (83 fields across host_control, sandbox_agent, streams, supervisor); (2) cross-file interface references surfaced as raw CapabilityPointer instead of typed RpcStub<T>; (3) the merged mod.ts barrel collided (five shared *_meta.ts export names, TS2308, plus 96 method param/result and domain type name collisions). All three are fixed in 0.2.0 by cross-file import lowering and the namespaced barrel; tests/unit/capnp/cross_file_roundtrip_test.ts now round-trips the cross-file composite through the GENERATED host_control/common codecs and a generated-interface client/server ping, and tools/check_wire.ts enforces byte-identical regeneration plus strict typecheck of all six committed schemas on every toolchain-present run.

compiledProbe.runtime: jsr:@nullstyle/capnp@0.3.0 (WASM session core embedded via the package's static import of generated/capnp_deno.wasm, 231702 bytes)

targets.aarch64-apple-darwin: executed: PROBE_OK against 0.3.0 (native; the 0.2.0 run additionally proved empty DENO_DIR + dead proxy WASM embedding)
targets.aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu: executed: PROBE_OK inside the fc-smoke Lima VM at the 0.2.0 adoption (Ubuntu, kernel 6.8, Deno 2.9.1 artifact) with empty DENO_DIR; not yet re-run against 0.3.0
targets.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu: compile-only on this host; execution proof lands with x86_64 CI (see PLAN M12)

compiledProbe.vsockFlagVerdict: Deno 2.9.x requires --unstable-vsock for Deno.listen/Deno.connect with transport 'vsock' (probed on guest Deno 2.9.2 aarch64-linux with vsock_loopback: no flag and --unstable-net both fail with 'The --unstable-vsock flag must be provided'); studioboxd and its in-guest tests must pass --unstable-vsock until the API stabilizes
